attachments.notes: Fictional example attachment used only to demonstrate the workflow.

positioning.value: A reusable wearable that guides desk workers through short, discreet posture-reset exercises.

target_user.value: Desk workers who experience recurring neck and shoulder discomfort during long computer sessions.

problem.value: Existing posture reminders are easy to ignore and often interrupt focused work without showing a corrective action.

solution.value: PulsePatch senses sustained upper-back posture and delivers a gentle directional vibration that cues a 30-second reset movement.

value: Directional haptics turn a passive alert into an immediately actionable movement cue
A removable electronics pod allows the textile carrier to be washed and reused

competitive_difference.value: Unlike screen notifications or camera-based trackers, PulsePatch gives private on-body guidance and processes motion data on the device.

user_benefit.value: Users receive a specific corrective cue without leaving the current task or enabling a camera.

client_brand_benefit.value: The modular architecture gives the client a serviceable wearable platform with replaceable textile parts instead of a sealed single-use product.

social_impact.value: The design makes preventive movement guidance accessible during ordinary desk work without recording video of the user or workplace.

sustainability.value: The washable carrier and replaceable battery-and-sensor pod separate high-wear textile parts from longer-life electronics.

aesthetics_form.value: A low-profile curved pod follows the upper back, while the seamless grey textile carrier is visually quiet under everyday clothing.

cmf.value: Matte recycled-polycarbonate pod, neutral grey knitted carrier, and soft silicone skin-contact pads.

physical_dimensions.value: Electronics pod: 72 × 38 × 9 mm; textile carrier: 180 × 95 mm
